description: First-3-turn sequences extracted from real tournament play. Indexed by {deck_archetype, going_first}. Multiple-source matches strengthen confidence — entries with sample_count: 1 are observation, not yet canonical.

turn_1.supporter_target: search up to 3 Basic Team Rocket Pokémon to HAND (not bench)

items.purpose: tutor key Pokémon to hand; ideal pitch is a basic energy if any in hand
search a TR supporter (ideally Team Rocket's Ariana for T2)

energy_attach.energy: Team Rocket's Energy (special, counts as 2 energy)

turn_1.ends_turn_with: 1 active TR basic, 1+ benched TR basic, ~1 supporter banked in hand for T2 (Ariana), at least 1 special TR Energy attached

turn_2.supporter_value: draws to 8 if all in-play are TR Pokémon (5-card draw if not)
turn_2.ideal_secondary: evolve a Tarountula→Spidops if possible, then Spidops Charging Up to attach a basic energy from discard, then Rocket Rush attack scaling with TR Pokémon in play (typical 90-150 dmg early)

turn_3.primary_supporter: Team Rocket's Giovanni or Team Rocket's Archer
turn_3.purpose: Giovanni gust + draw 2; or Archer 5-card refill
turn_3.follow_up: Mewtwo ex Psydrive line: 1 charged Spidops bench + Mewtwo manual energy + special TR Energy = 160-220 damage range

entries.notes: T1 Proton-to-HAND (not bench) is critical — preserves Ariana scaling next turn. The biggest archetype anti-pattern: shipping TR Pokémon to bench via search instead of hand.

turn_1_or_2_setup.supporter_value: shuffle hand to deck and draw 8 — best when paired with Meowth ex 'turn-into-Lillie's' line

items.purpose: discard pitch fuels later Blaziken Charging Up + Crispin tutors

energy_attach.energy: Psychic or Fire (often via Crispin tutor)

turn_1_or_2_setup.active_choice: Budew → Itchy Pollen for item lock (NOT Phantom Dive turn) — slow archetype, stall while building Stage 2

turn_3_target.ideal_state: 1 Drakloak evolved, 1+ Torchic-Combusken line developing, 1 charged Dragapult prep, Munkidori benched for Adrena-Brain math
turn_3_target.first_real_attack: Phantom Dive turn 4 (or turn 5 if no Rare Candy) — pre-empts opp by gusting their unpowered key threat with Boss's Orders

going_first_variant.rationale: Choose to go FIRST despite slower setup, to deny opp's T1 Proton search — eat the T1 Phantom Dive ban in exchange
going_first_variant.trade: lose the T1 Lillie/Poffin tempo but gain massive disruption to opp's archetype-defining T1

items.purpose: search any non-rule-box Pokémon — typically Drakloak (evolution prep) or Dudunsparce (engine)

bench: (optional) Dudunsparce — Run-Away Draw nonex draw engine
turn_1.ends_turn_with: active Dreepy (energy attached), 2-3 bench Pokémon, no supporter played, Drakloak in hand for T2 evolve

turn_2.ideal_secondary: evolve Dreepy → Drakloak (active or bench), use Recon Directive (top-2 look, take 1, bottom 1) to filter for Phantom Dive pieces (Rare Candy / Dragapult ex / Fire energy)

turn_3.primary_action: Phantom Dive (200 + 60 spread) — Rare Candy → Dragapult ex on a charged Drakloak
turn_3.alternative: If matchup is Crustle (EX-immune wall), prioritize Dudunsparce ex line (Hero's Cape on Dudunsparce + 3-energy Destructive Drill ignores effects)
turn_3.supporter: Boss's Orders to gust opp's setup Pokémon (Roselia, Dwebble, Munkidori, Drakloak)

entries.notes: ACE SPEC choice — Hero's Cape preferred over Unfair Stamp. Hero's Cape on Dudunsparce (1-prize) is mathematically superior to Hero's Cape on Dragapult (2-prize) because it forces opp to invest 2 attacks (240+ dmg) for 1 prize. Erratic Machinations supporter for hand-discard reset (Mateusz famously discarded 16 cards in finals Game 2).
entries.anti_pattern: Don't lead Dragapult line if opp is Crustle — switch to Dudunsparce ex line for Destructive Drill bypass.

turn_1.primary_supporter: Lillie's Determination OR Pokégear 3.0 (Pokégear searches a supporter — chain into Lillie's)

energy_attach.to: active Dwebble or Cornerstone Mask Ogerpon ex

turn_1.active_choice: Dwebble (tank Ascension setup) OR Cornerstone Mask Ogerpon ex (immune to ability damage)
turn_1.key_decision: PASS on Ascension (Dwebble's deck-search-and-evolve attack) when energy attach efficiency matters more — observed across all 3 sources

turn_2.primary_supporter: Hilda OR Brock's Scouting OR Pokémon Center Lady (toolbox supporters, all chain via Pokégear)
turn_2.ideal_secondary: evolve Dwebble → Crustle on a 2-energy Dwebble (manual + T1 attach + T2 attach)
turn_2.tool_attach: Hero's Cape on first Crustle (150 + 100 = 250 HP, immune to EX damage)

turn_3_or_later.wall_phase: Stack Growing Grass Energy (HP +20 per copy) and Spiky Energy (counter damage). Healing via Pokémon Center Lady, Colress's Tenacity (stadium chain). Final HP often 290-330.
turn_3_or_later.attack_phase: Superb Scissors (Crustle attack) only when opp is at 1-2 prizes — until then, just wall and use Boss's Orders to gust opp's setup Pokémon.
turn_3_or_later.backup_attacker: Mega Kangaskhan ex rapid-fire combo (coin-flip per energy attached, 100 per heads — 200/300 with 2-3 heads). Used as the 'Crustle can't reach' answer (e.g. vs anti-EX walls). 3-prize Pokémon — only commit when opp can't easily 1HKO.

entries.notes: Crustle's plan is INVERTED from typical aggro: do nothing for 2-3 turns, build energy + heal, never attack until opp is at 1-2 prizes. AI archetype playbook should encode 'wall first, attack last' as a phase transition triggered by opp's prize count.
entries.anti_pattern: Don't waste energy on Cornerstone Mask Ogerpon ex's attack — it's a tank, not an attacker. Don't commit Mega Kangaskhan early — it's a 3-prize liability when opp can hit it.

items.purpose: tutor 4 basics: Cynthia's Gible x2-3 + Cynthia's Roselia x1-2

turn_2.ideal_secondary: evolve Cynthia's Gible → Cynthia's Gabite (Cynthia's Gabite has tutor ability to find more Gabite + Roserade), evolve Cynthia's Roselia → Cynthia's Roserade (energy-acceleration ability)

turn_3.primary_action: Evolve Cynthia's Gabite → Cynthia's Garchomp, attack with Corkscrew Dive (attack + draw cards) OR Dragon Slice (high damage)
turn_3.supporter: Boss's Orders to gust setup Pokémon, OR another Cynthia for energy refill
turn_3.ace_spec: Unfair Stamp (Netti's choice, called out as 'variant-defining' over Neo Upper Energy)

entries.notes: Cynthia's Garchomp's signature engine is the Cynthia's Gabite tutor + Cynthia's Roserade energy ramp. Boss's Orders down to a 2-card hand is acceptable because Corkscrew Dive draws cards next turn — Engine's trainer evaluation should weight 'attacker-with-draw-effect' as a hand-recovery guarantee.
entries.anti_pattern: Don't skimp on Cynthia's Roselia — energy ramp is the deck's only path to powering Garchomp on schedule.

items.purpose: search any Maman Pokémon — item-lock immune via stadium category

turn_1.ends_turn_with: Snorunt active, bench stocked, stadium up, Munkidori with 1 energy

turn_2.primary_supporter: Lillie's Determination (shuffle + draw 8) OR Tatsugiri Order Up (look at top 6, take 1 supporter — if Tatsugiri active)
turn_2.ideal_secondary: evolve Snorunt → Frostlass (Freezing Shroud passive: 10 dmg per turn to all ability Pokémon), evolve Maman's Impidimp → Maman's Morgrem

turn_3.primary_action: Evolve Maman's Morgrem → Maman's Grimmsnarl ex. Activate Punk Up ability (search 1 Dark Energy from deck, attach to Maman's Pokémon — energy acceleration!)
turn_3.attack: Shadow Bullet (180 active + 30 bench) for first prize. Munkidori Adrena-Brain to set up future bench KOs.
turn_3.supporter: Petrel (Order Up tutor target — tutored from Tatsugiri T1 line)

entries.notes: Maman's Tatsugiri Order Up + Petrel chain is the archetype's signature setup. Item-lock vulnerability is mitigated by Spike Muff Gym (stadium-search). Frostlass Freezing Shroud is bidirectional damage — hits own ability Pokémon too — so deck needs Munkidori healing or HP buffer tools.
entries.anti_pattern: Don't promote Budew when opp has no out (e.g. Dragapult mirror) — Itchy Pollen value drops to zero. Mateusz/Nicklas G1 turn 4 demonstrated this (Nicklas chose Tatsugiri Order Up over Itchy Pollen).

items.purpose: places 2 dmg counters per turn on opp ability Pokémon — KEY accelerator

turn_2.ideal_secondary: evolve Staryu → Mega Starmie ex (with Crispin attach 2 different energies — typically 1 to attacker, 1 to bench); evolve Snorunt → Frostlass / Mega Frostlass
turn_2.first_attack: Mega Starmie ex Jetting Blow (120 active + 50 bench)

turn_3.primary_action: Repeat Jetting Blow OR pivot to Mega Frostlass for opp-hand-size scaling damage (50 × opp hand cards — devastating after opp Lillie/Iono)
turn_3.supporter: Boss's Orders to convert spread damage into KOs

entries.notes: Risky Ruins is the deck's signature accelerator — turns 50-dmg bench snipe into 70-dmg KO range against evolving basics. Mega Frostlass is the late-game hand-size punisher. Distinct from mega-starmie-dusknoir (which uses Cursed Blast for Crustle counter).

candidate_lines_to_validate.line: T3 Giovanni gust → push opp big-attacker to bench → Mewtwo Psydrive on whatever's promoted
T2 spread Crispin energies across 2 Drakloaks rather than committing to 1 — defuses Boss's/Giovanni gust
Anti-Crustle pivot — when opp is detected as crustle, abandon Dragapult line on T2-T3 and route through Dudunsparce ex Destructive Drill instead. Hero's Cape on Dudunsparce.
T2 PASS on Ascension when opp lacks T1 pressure — observed in 2 of 3 source matches. Energy efficiency > Crustle rush.
Mega Kangaskhan ex rapid-fire as anti-EX-immune-wall counter (e.g. vs Mega Starmie). Coin-flip variance — high reward, high variance.
Cursed Blast + Jetting Blow exact-OHKO combo on EX Pokémon — bypasses Mysterious Rocking via ability damage.
Corkscrew Dive attack-then-draw enables aggressive supporter pre-commitment (Boss's Orders down to 2-card hand)
